name: Margareth Menezes
description:
'''Margareth Menezes da Purificação''' (born 13 October 1962) is a Brazilian singer and politician from [[Salvador, Bahia|Salvador]], Bahia. She currently serves as [[Minister of Culture (Brazil)|Minister of Culture]] under [[Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva]].

Her style is considered [[axé]] but her music also steers into [[samba]] and [[Música popular brasileira|MPB]], at times drawing on African rhythms and reggae.

Menezes is best known in Brazil for the debut single "Faraó (Divindade do Egito)" 1988 of [[Olodum]] and for "Me Abraça e Me Beija", a major hit in 1990. She also scored another hit with "Dandalunda", a song which reached notable popularity during the 2003 Salvador carnival.
